data.narrative:
Reverion, a Series B climate and energy company, was last valued at $310M post-money after raising $79M to date. The peer universe comprises five same-stage, same-sector private companies spanning $120M to $5.0B in valuation, alongside one public comparable. The peer cohort includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $589M. Peer-median analysis across the five-company sample produced $400M, reflecting the wide dispersion inherent in frontier-tech valuations (range: $120M–$5.0B). The Commonwealth Fusion Systems outlier at $5.0B reflects nuclear fusion's capital intensity and investor appetite but limited comparability on stage-specific risk and commercialization timeline.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $340M (low) to $677M (high), with a median of $494M. The $310M current valuation sits below this range, suggesting modest upside potential if the company has achieved material technical or commercial milestones since last funding. Key uncertainties center on technology maturity, path to unit economics, customer validation progress, and sector-wide capital availability. The extreme peer range signals valuation sensitivity to differentiation claims and risk tolerance among investors in this sector.
